description: Cross-reference map of equivalent, related, and referenced standards across jurisdictions and frameworks. Each mapping has a relationship type and a bidirectional flag indicating whether the relationship applies in both directions.

mappings.to: SP 800-53 Rev. 5 — Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations
mappings.scope: ISMS requirements vs control catalog; partial overlap — ISO 27001 is certifiable, SP 800-53 is mandatory for US federal
ISMS certification vs attestation; overlapping but not identical scopes

mappings.to: Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS) — official site
mappings.scope: Indian national adoption of ISO/IEC 27001
NIST CSF is a risk framework; ISO 27001 is an ISMS standard. Often implemented together

mappings.from: PCI DSS — Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard
mappings.scope: PCI DSS is cardholder-data-specific; ISO 27001 is broader. Organizations often certify both

mappings.to: SP 800-53 Rev. 5 — Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations
mappings.scope: FedRAMP authorizes cloud services using SP 800-53 control baselines

mappings.from: Federal Information Security Modernization Act (FISMA) background
mappings.scope: RMF is the process that implements FISMA requirements
NIS2 covers organisational cyber resilience; CRA covers product-level security for digital products
DORA is financial-sector-specific; NIS2 is horizontal. DORA takes precedence for financial entities
WCAG 2.2 adds 1 new success criterion over 2.1
WCAG 2.1 extends 2.0; ISO 40500 is the ISO adoption of 2.0

mappings.from: EN 301 549 v3.2.1 — Accessibility requirements for ICT products and services
mappings.scope: EN 301 549 incorporates WCAG 2.1 AA as its web content conformance baseline

mappings.from: ADA Title II Web & Mobile App Accessibility Rule (2024)
mappings.scope: DOJ rule mandates WCAG 2.1 AA conformance for state and local government web/mobile

mappings.to: EN 301 549 v3.2.1 — Accessibility requirements for ICT products and services
mappings.scope: Canadian adoption of EN 301 549:2021

mappings.from: Public Sector Bodies (Websites and Mobile Applications) (No. 2) Accessibility Regulations 2018
mappings.scope: UK regulations require WCAG 2.2 AA conformance (amended 2022 from 2.1)

mappings.to: EN 301 549 v3.2.1 — Accessibility requirements for ICT products and services
mappings.scope: EN 301 549 is the harmonised standard supporting EAA conformity
UK GDPR mirrors EU GDPR post-Brexit with UK-specific modifications

mappings.to: Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 (DPDP Act)
mappings.scope: DPDP is India's GDPR-equivalent; different scope (digital only) but similar data-principal rights
ISO 27701 extends ISO 27001 for privacy management; supports GDPR compliance
California privacy law; narrower than GDPR but similar data-subject rights
Virginia state privacy law; similar to CCPA/CPRA

mappings.from: Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 (DPDP Act)
mappings.to: IT (Reasonable Security Practices and Sensitive Personal Data or Information) Rules, 2011 (SPDI Rules)
mappings.scope: DPDP Act is India's comprehensive data protection law; SPDI Rules under IT Act remain for non-digital data

mappings.to: AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0), NIST AI 100-1
mappings.scope: EU AI Act is binding law; NIST AI RMF is voluntary framework. Both address AI risk management
ISO 42001 provides certifiable AI management system; supports EU AI Act compliance

mappings.from: Colorado AI Act — SB 24-205, Consumer Protections for Artificial Intelligence
mappings.scope: First US state comprehensive AI law; narrower than EU AI Act but similar risk-based approach
R3 is the current ICH revision; the generic GCP entry and R2 are superseded by R3

mappings.from: ICD-11 — International Classification of Diseases, 11th revision
mappings.to: ICD-10 — International Classification of Diseases, 10th revision
mappings.scope: ICD-11 is the current global standard; ICD-10 still in use in many countries
FHIR is the modern API-based standard; v2 remains widely deployed. Both coexist
EU and US premarket pathways for medical devices; different mechanisms, same purpose

mappings.from: ISO 13485 — Medical devices, quality management systems
mappings.scope: ISO 13485 is international QMS for medical devices; 21 CFR 820 is US FDA QSR. FDA now aligns with ISO 13485

mappings.from: Basel III: international regulatory framework for banks
mappings.scope: Basel Framework is the BIS consolidated online version of Basel III; Basel IV finalisation in progress
Both address financial-sector operational resilience and cyber; DORA is EU, RBI is India

mappings.from: Cybersecurity and Cyber Resilience Framework (CSCRF) for SEBI Regulated Entities
mappings.to: CERT-In Directions under Section 70B, IT Act (28 Apr 2022)
mappings.scope: SEBI CSCRF requires alignment with CERT-In incident reporting timelines

mappings.from: CERT-In Directions under Section 70B, IT Act (28 Apr 2022)
mappings.scope: Both mandate cyber incident reporting; CERT-In is India (6-hour), NIS2 is EU (24-hour initial, 72-hour detailed)

mappings.to: Cybersecurity and Cyber Resilience Framework (CSCRF) for SEBI Regulated Entities
mappings.scope: Both are Indian sectoral cyber frameworks; RBI for banks, SEBI for capital markets
Digital India Act will replace the IT Act 2000 when enacted
IATF 16949 extends ISO 9001 for automotive industry

mappings.from: European Sustainability Reporting Standards (ESRS) — Delegated Regulation (EU) 2023/2772
mappings.scope: ESRS incorporates TCFD recommendations into mandatory EU reporting

mappings.from: GHS — Globally Harmonized System of Classification and Labelling of Chemicals
mappings.scope: GHS covers classification/labelling; REACH covers registration/authorisation. Both apply to chemicals in the EU
27001 defines ISMS requirements; 27002 provides control implementation guidance

mappings.from: ISO/IEC 27017:2015 — Information security controls for cloud services
mappings.scope: 27017 extends 27001/27002 for cloud-specific controls

mappings.from: ISO/IEC 27018:2019 — Protection of PII in public clouds
mappings.scope: 27018 is cloud-PII-specific; 27701 is broader PIMS. Both address privacy
Section 508 refreshed 2018 to align with WCAG 2.0 AA
accname defines how accessible names are computed from ARIA semantics
Both govern health data; HIPAA is US sectoral, GDPR Art.9 is EU comprehensive
EHDS builds on GDPR for primary and secondary use of health data in the EU

mappings.to: Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 (DPDP Act)
mappings.scope: Aadhaar Act governs biometric identity; DPDP governs personal data processing. Both apply to Aadhaar data
Both govern personal financial data; GDPR for EU, GLBA for US financial institutions
AI Act references GDPR for processing of personal data in AI systems; high-risk AI processing must comply with GDPR
Both govern processing of personal data in clinical trials; CTR references GDPR for health data processing
AI-enabled medical devices must comply with both AI Act high-risk requirements and MDR conformity assessment
AI/ML SaMD falls under AI Act high-risk classification alongside medical device regulation
AI Act high-risk AI systems used in financial services require risk management overlapping SOX controls
CRA covers cybersecurity of digital products including AI systems; AI Act requires cybersecurity measures for high-risk AI
DORA is financial-sector-specific and takes precedence over NIS2 for financial entities; NIS2 covers other critical sectors

mappings.to: IEC 61508-1:2010 — Functional safety of E/E/PE safety-related systems
mappings.scope: NIS2 cyber risk management applies to manufacturing essential entities; IEC 61508 addresses functional safety including cyber threats
Both require cyber risk management in healthcare; NIS2 covers EU health providers, HIPAA covers US covered entities
Same regulation listed under two domains with different name formatting; cybersecurity and financial-services perspectives
CRA sets cybersecurity requirements for products with digital elements; Machinery Regulation covers safety of machinery including software
Both mandate corporate disclosure; CSRD for sustainability, SOX for financial controls. Overlapping reporting obligations for EU-listed US companies
EMS under ISO 14001 provides operational data feeding CSRD/ESRS environmental disclosures

mappings.from: PCI DSS — Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard
mappings.scope: Same standard referenced from two domains; cybersecurity perspective and financial-services payments perspective

mappings.to: AS9100D — Quality Management Systems: Requirements for Aviation, Space, and Defense Organizations
mappings.scope: AS9100 extends ISO 9001 for aviation, space and defense organizations

mappings.to: ISO 13485:2016 — Medical devices — Quality management systems
mappings.scope: ISO 13485 adapts ISO 9001 principles for medical device QMS with regulatory requirements

mappings.to: ISO/IEC 90003:2018 — Guidelines for applying ISO 9001 to software
mappings.scope: ISO/IEC 90003 provides guidelines for applying ISO 9001 to software development

mappings.to: Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive (CSDDD) — Directive (EU) 2024/1760
mappings.scope: CSDDD due diligence covers chemical safety; REACH compliance is part of environmental due diligence under CSDDD

mappings.from: GHS — Globally Harmonized System of Classification and Labelling of Chemicals
mappings.to: CLP Regulation (EC) No 1272/2008 — Classification, Labelling and Packaging
mappings.scope: CLP implements GHS in EU law; GHS is the global framework, CLP is the EU regulation applying it

mappings.from: CLP Regulation (EC) No 1272/2008 — Classification, Labelling and Packaging
mappings.to: ADR — Agreement concerning the International Carriage of Dangerous Goods by Road
mappings.scope: CLP classification determines dangerous goods categories for transport under ADR

mappings.from: 21 CFR Part 11 — Electronic Records; Electronic Signatures
mappings.scope: Part 11 electronic records requirements apply to QSR quality system records for medical devices

mappings.from: 21 CFR Part 11 — Electronic Records; Electronic Signatures
mappings.scope: Part 11 applies to electronic GxP records; GAMP provides risk-based approach for compliant computerized systems

mappings.from: GAMP 5 (Second Edition) — A Risk-Based Approach to Compliant GxP Computerized Systems
mappings.scope: GAMP 5 provides the risk-based approach for GxP computerized systems referenced in pharmaceutical GMP

mappings.from: GAMP 5 (Second Edition) — A Risk-Based Approach to Compliant GxP Computerized Systems
mappings.to: IEC 62304:2006+AMD1:2015 — Medical device software life cycle processes
mappings.scope: Both address software lifecycle validation; GAMP for GxP systems, IEC 62304 for medical device software
FHIR resources support SaMD data exchange and interoperability for connected medical devices
FHIR implementation follows software lifecycle processes; 12207 provides the process framework

mappings.from: IEC 62304 — Medical device software life cycle processes
mappings.scope: IEC 62304 provides medical-device-specific software lifecycle; ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207 is the general software lifecycle standard

mappings.from: ISO/IEC 25010:2023 — Systems and software quality models (SQuaRE)
mappings.to: IEC 62304:2006+AMD1:2015 — Medical device software life cycle processes
mappings.scope: SQuaRE quality model provides quality characteristics framework applied to medical device software under IEC 62304

mappings.from: SR 11-7 — Guidance on Model Risk Management
mappings.scope: SR 11-7 model risk management supports SOX internal controls for financial models used in reporting
SOX IT general controls for financial reporting align with NIST CSF security controls
FTC enforces Section 5 for unfair/deceptive practices including financial privacy under GLBA Safeguards Rule
FTC uses Section 5 to enforce reasonable data security; NIST CSF provides the framework for reasonable security
GPSR covers general product safety including cybersecurity risks; CRA provides specific cybersecurity requirements for digital products

mappings.from: International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR, 22 CFR 120–130)
mappings.scope: ITAR export controls require secure supply chain processes; CTPAT provides supply chain security framework for imports
EAR controls US dual-use exports; UCC governs EU customs procedures. Both apply to cross-border trade compliance
Wassenaar controls dual-use export licensing; HS codes classify goods for customs including controlled items
OFAC sanctions require screening in trade transactions; UCC customs procedures intersect with sanctions compliance at borders

mappings.from: Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive (CSDDD) — Directive (EU) 2024/1760
mappings.to: UN Guiding Principles on Business and Human Rights
mappings.scope: CSDDD codifies the UNGPs into EU law; requires due diligence aligned with the UN framework

mappings.to: UN Guiding Principles on Business and Human Rights
mappings.scope: UK Modern Slavery Act reporting aligns with UNGP human rights due diligence expectations

mappings.to: UN Guiding Principles on Business and Human Rights
mappings.scope: UFLPA forced labor prevention aligns with UNGP human rights due diligence for supply chains

mappings.to: UN Guiding Principles on Business and Human Rights
mappings.scope: Conflict minerals reporting supports UNGP human rights due diligence for mineral supply chains

mappings.to: Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act
mappings.scope: Conflict minerals rule is Section 1502 of the Dodd-Frank Act
ISO 14001 EMS data supports GRI environmental reporting; EMS provides the management system, GRI provides the disclosure framework

mappings.from: ISO 14064-1:2018 — Greenhouse gases — Organization-level quantification and reporting
mappings.to: GHG Protocol Corporate Accounting and Reporting Standard
mappings.scope: Both provide GHG accounting at organization level; ISO 14064-1 is the standard, GHG Protocol is the methodology

mappings.to: Markets in Financial Instruments Directive (MiFID II), Directive 2014/65/EU
mappings.scope: SFDR sustainability disclosures apply to financial products distributed under MiFID II; both govern EU financial services transparency
SEC climate disclosure rule requires climate-related disclosures in SEC filings; SOX governs internal controls over financial reporting including these disclosures

mappings.from: IEC 62304 — Medical device software life cycle processes
mappings.to: IEC 62304:2006+AMD1:2015 — Medical device software life cycle processes
mappings.scope: Same standard listed under two domains with different name formatting; healthcare and medical-devices perspectives

mappings.from: ISO 13485 — Medical devices, quality management systems
mappings.to: ISO 13485:2016 — Medical devices — Quality management systems
mappings.scope: Same standard listed under two domains with different name formatting; healthcare and medical-devices perspectives
Same regulation listed under two names in the ai-governance domain; one uses the formal title, the other the common short name

mappings.from: AS9100D — Quality Management Systems: Requirements for Aviation, Space, and Defense Organizations
mappings.to: AS9100D — Quality management systems — Requirements for aviation, space and defense organizations
mappings.scope: Same standard listed under two domains with different name formatting; aerospace-defense and manufacturing perspectives

mappings.from: Directive (EU) 2024/2881 — Ambient Air Quality and Cleaner Air for Europe
mappings.scope: The EU 2024 recast tightens limit values toward the WHO 2021 health-based guideline levels

mappings.to: Directive 2008/50/EC — Ambient Air Quality and Cleaner Air for Europe
mappings.scope: The UK regulations transpose the EU ambient air quality directives into domestic law
